title: sambucus pubens f. dissecta
intro: Sambucus pubens f. dissecta, also known as the American elderberry shrub or elderflower, is a deciduous shrub or small tree belonging to the Adoxaceae family. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in its flowers, leaves, and berries, which contain bioactive compounds such as flavonoids, anthocyanins, and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Sambucus pubens f. dissecta include its anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory infections. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, infusions, and syrups, and are often used to boost the immune system and promote overall well-being.
